message:
feat(ufunc): implement var, std, argmin, and argmax reductions

Add four new reduction operations to UFuncArray matching NumPy semantics:

reduce_var(axis, keepdims, ddof):
- Population variance with configurable degrees of freedom
- Full axis support including None (flattened), specific axis, keepdims
- Two-pass algorithm: compute means then sum of squared deviations
- Output dtype follows promote_for_mean_reduction (int→F64)

reduce_std(axis, keepdims, ddof):
- Standard deviation via sqrt of variance, delegates to reduce_var

reduce_argmin(axis) / reduce_argmax(axis):
- Return index of min/max value along axis, output dtype always I64
- Flat-index mode when axis=None, per-slice indices when axis specified
- New generic reduce_argfold_axis_contiguous helper with comparator fn

Supporting infrastructure:
- reduce_var_axis_contiguous: optimized strided variance accumulation
- reduce_argfold_axis_contiguous: generic argmin/argmax inner loop

Tests (16 new test cases):
- var: axis=None, ddof=1, axis=1, keepdims, dtype promotion
- std: axis=None, axis=0
- argmin: axis=None, axis=0, axis=1
- argmax: axis=None, axis=0, axis=1, 3D axis=1

Also applies let-chain refactor in workflow_scenarios.rs and
minor rustfmt cleanup in fnp-dtype imports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
